note: The official 2025-2026 Handbook publishes each JS2020 Major on a separate Programme page; the five local Majors are stored as separate supplements and shared courses are repeated only across their respective Major identities. All five pages require 54 units of Major Courses and a 6-unit Honours/Capstone Project. Chinese Language and Literature requires eight electives with at least three from Group A, two from Group C and one each from Groups B and D. Creative and Professional Writing requires 18 elective units and states that students take at least two courses from two different modules; its displayed Either/Or pairs are retained. English Language and Literature requires eight electives, at least four at Level 3 or 4; an optional concentration requires at least six electives under that concentration and does not appear on the transcript. Its uncoded 3-unit Third Language option (Putonghua excluded) is an open option and is not fabricated as a coded course. Humanities students seeking a concentration use 18 major-elective units plus 3 Free Elective units for 21 units in one group, with the remaining 6 major-elective units taken as two courses in the other group; students without a concentration take at least three courses from each group and 24 units total. Translation requires eight electives, at least three at Level 3 or above. Minor title differences are resolved against linked 2025-2026 course pages; Chinese and LANG course pages additionally display Chinese titles, while the English Programme-table titles are retained. The 2026-2027 Handbook endpoint is currently protected by HTTP 401 Basic Authentication and is not represented as verified. University Language, General Education and uncoded Free Electives are excluded. No Year or Term is assigned.
